question: According to Lecture 1, which phrase is the business-focused definition of marketing?

answers.A: A production system for reducing costs and increasing efficiency.
answers.B: A short-term sales tactic focused only on price cuts.
answers.C: A distribution process for moving products to retailers.
answers.D: The set of strategies and activities by which companies acquire and engage customers, build strong customer relationships, and create superior customer value in order to capture value from customers in return.

question: According to Lecture 1, which is Goal 1 of marketing?

answers.A: Set the lowest possible price in the market
answers.B: Help drive long-term company profitability by recapturing some of the delivered value from satisfied and loyal customers
answers.C: Attract new customers by promising superior value
answers.D: Keep and grow current customers by delivering the promised value

question: According to Lecture 1, which is Goal 2 of marketing?

answers.A: Attract new customers by promising superior value
answers.B: Reduce advertising to improve margins
answers.C: Help drive long-term company profitability by recapturing some of the delivered value from satisfied and loyal customers
answers.D: Keep and grow current customers by delivering the promised value

question: According to Lecture 1, which is Goal 3 of marketing?

answers.A: Attract new customers by promising superior value
answers.B: Keep and grow current customers by delivering the promised value
answers.C: Help drive long-term company profitability by recapturing some of the delivered value from satisfied and loyal customers
answers.D: Match competitor prices in every category

question: In Lecture 1, which exact phrase defines a need?

answers.A: Needs shaped by culture & individual personality
answers.B: Human wants backed by buying power
answers.C: Easy, instant, mobile messaging
answers.D: State of felt deprivation

question: In Lecture 1, which exact phrase defines a want?

answers.A: State of felt deprivation
answers.B: Connect with others
answers.C: Human wants backed by buying power
answers.D: Needs shaped by culture & individual personality

question: In Lecture 1, which exact phrase defines demand?

answers.A: Needs shaped by culture & individual personality
answers.B: State of felt deprivation
answers.C: Human wants backed by buying power
answers.D: Connect with others

question: In Lecture 1, which example is listed for a want?

answers.A: Connect with others
answers.B: Creating and exchanging value with others
answers.C: Easy, instant, mobile messaging
answers.D: Free messaging application, adopted globally

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact phrase defines the marketing environment?

answers.A: The marketing environment includes the actors and forces outside marketing that affect marketing management’s ability to build and maintain successful relationships with target customers.
answers.B: Customer insights is a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior.
answers.C: Consumer buyer behavior is how people decide what to buy, use, and recommend.
answers.D: Marketing channel: A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.

question: Which exact Lecture 2 phrase completes the slide note under the marketing environment?

answers.A: The full mix of benefits upon which a brand is positioned
answers.B: The desired customer service level at the lowest total cost
answers.C: Everything outside of your product that can help or hurt demand
answers.D: The end-to-end customer journey

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under microenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Technological: platform shifts, AI, cybersecurity threats, new standards
answers.B: Demographic: who your users are (age, income, location…)
answers.C: Natural/Environmental: climate risk, energy costs, sustainability
answers.D: Marketing Intermediaries (how you reach customers): app stores, resellers, agencies, marketplaces

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under microenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Demographic: who your users are (age, income, location…)
answers.B: Cultural/Social: values, trust, ethics, privacy attitudes
answers.C: Economic: budgets, inflation, interest rates, hiring freezes
answers.D: Public (groups that shape reputation): media, online communities, regulators, investors

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under macroenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Competitors (and substitutes): direct rivals + 'do nothing' or spreadsheets
answers.B: Customers (buyers & users): needs, budgets, switching costs, decision-makers
answers.C: Economic: budgets, inflation, interest rates, hiring freezes
answers.D: Marketing Intermediaries (how you reach customers): app stores, resellers, agencies, marketplaces

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under macroenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Customers (buyers & users): needs, budgets, switching costs, decision-makers
answers.B: Public (groups that shape reputation): media, online communities, regulators, investors
answers.C: Company (internal teams): product, engineering sales, support
answers.D: Political/Legal: privacy, IP, accessibility, competition laws

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact phrase appears under why consumer behavior matters?

answers.A: To understand how consumers make decisions
answers.B: To coordinate communication tools to deliver a clear, consistent, and compelling brand message.
answers.C: To add offerings at a new price/market level.
answers.D: To deliver the desired customer service level at the lowest total cost.

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact phrase appears under consumer behavior in tech?

answers.A: Exporting: selling products produced in one country to buyers in another
answers.B: Advocacy: What makes people leave reviews, share, and recommend?
answers.C: Trialability: sandbox demo and a 7-day free trial
answers.D: Awareness: introduce new products or build primary demand

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact sentence appears on the customer insights slide?

answers.A: Price is the money charged for a product/service.
answers.B: One brand should feel like one voice, no matter where the customer encounters it.
answers.C: Advertising is paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services.
answers.D: Data tells you what happened. Insight tells you why. Strategy tells you what to do.

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase defines customer insights?

answers.A: Customer-perceived value refers to benefits relative to costs versus competing offers
answers.B: Product: anything offered to satisfy a need or want
answers.C: Customer Insights is a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior
answers.D: A market segment is a group of buyers with similar needs/characteristics

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase appears under big data?

answers.A: Big data is a short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service
answers.B: Big data is the specific blend of promotion tools the company uses
answers.C: Big data is any information previously collected for another purpose
answers.D: Big data is high-volume, high-velocity, high-variety data from digital systems

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact item is listed as a source under big data?

answers.A: product usage analytics, transactions, CRM, support tickets, social reviews
answers.B: TV, digital, radio etc.
answers.C: discounts, coupons, displays, demonstrations, events
answers.D: mobile-first and short-form video discovery

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact sentence appears on the managing marketing info slide?

answers.A: Insight without action is trivia.
answers.B: Good logos are simple, readable and scalable.
answers.C: One brand should feel like one voice.
answers.D: Sell value, not price.

question: According to Lecture 3, which three sources are listed under developing marketing information?

answers.A: Internal Data – what customers do; Marketing Intelligence – what’s happening outside the company; Marketing Research – what customers say + why they do it
answers.B: Product – price – place
answers.C: Need – want – demand
answers.D: Advertising – remind customers; PR – engage publics; digital – go global

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase defines primary data?

answers.A: information previously collected for another purpose that can be used.
answers.B: the actors and forces outside marketing
answers.C: data you already collect from running the business and product
answers.D: information collected for the specific purpose at-hand.

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase defines secondary data?

answers.A: the process: Collect → clean → analyze → test → share → act
answers.B: a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior
answers.C: information previously collected for another purpose that can be used.
answers.D: information collected for the specific purpose at-hand.

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase defines a product?

answers.A: Advertising is paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services
answers.B: Product: anything offered to satisfy a need or want
answers.C: Price is the money charged for a product/service
answers.D: Marketing channel: A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase appears directly under the product definition?

answers.A: Includes wholesalers, retailers, distributors, and logistics providers
answers.B: Includes websites, social media, email, and mobile
answers.C: Includes goods, services, experiences, people, places, ideas
answers.D: Includes budgets, inflation, interest rates, hiring freezes

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase defines services?

answers.A: Services are the same as physical goods but cheaper
answers.B: Services are short-term incentives to encourage purchase
answers.C: Services are products sold only through digital channels
answers.D: Services are intangible benefits (you use them – you don’t own them)

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase appears on products, services, and experiences?

answers.A: Price is the only 'P' that produces revenue
answers.B: Informative advertising introduces new products or builds primary demand
answers.C: Experience is the differentiator (how it feels to buy / use / support)
answers.D: Competition laws shape markets and buyer behavior

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase labels the first product level?

answers.A: Product Mix Decisions = the full portfolio
answers.B: Actual Product: What is it, exactly?
answers.C: Core Value: What problem are we solving?
answers.D: Augmented Product: What makes it better to choose / use?

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase describes convenience products?

answers.A: Convenience: frequent, low effort
answers.B: Shopping: compare options
answers.C: Specialty: strong brand preference; high involvement
answers.D: Unsought: not actively looking; need awareness

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact sentence appears under B2B product classifications?

answers.A: Consumers only buy through direct channels
answers.B: B2B buyers care most about ROI, reliability, support & risk reduction
answers.C: Intermediaries exist to eliminate all costs
answers.D: B2C buyers care most about prestige, celebrity, and trend cycles

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact line appears under product attributes?

answers.A: Good attributes reduce friction → higher adoption & retention
answers.B: Good attributes guarantee market leadership
answers.C: Good attributes eliminate the need for promotion
answers.D: Good attributes reduce prices → higher margins only

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase distinguishes performance quality?

answers.A: Performance Quality: how well it works (speed, accuracy, features work)
answers.B: Conformance Quality: how consistently it works (bugs, uptime, reliability)
answers.C: Style: how it looks and feels (visual appeal)
answers.D: Design: how it works (usability + usefulness)

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase defines design?

answers.A: Style: how it looks and feels (visual appeal)
answers.B: Features: capabilities that solve the user’s job
answers.C: Design: how it works (usability + usefulness)
answers.D: Quality: reliability, performance, security

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase defines price?

answers.A: Price is the standard markup added to cost
answers.B: Price is whatever competitors charge
answers.C: Price is the money charged for a product/service – or more broadly, the total value customers exchange to receive the benefits (money + time + effort + risk)
answers.D: Price is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact sentence appears under important notes?

answers.A: Place is the only 'P' that produces revenue; the others create costs.
answers.B: Promotion is the only 'P' that produces revenue; the others create costs.
answers.C: Product is the only 'P' that produces revenue; the others create costs.
answers.D: Price is the only “P’ that produces revenue; the others (product, place, promotion) create costs.

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under customer-value based pricing?

answers.A: Set price based on the costs of producing, distributing, and selling
answers.B: Set price based on the customer’s perceived value (benefits), not the sellers cost
answers.C: Definition: Price = cost + standard markup
answers.D: Set price to cover costs (breakeven) or earn a target profit

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under customer-value based pricing?

answers.A: Customer-driven: start with what buyers value and will pay for
answers.B: Supplier-driven: start with input cost increases
answers.C: Competition-driven: start with what rivals charge and copy it
answers.D: Promotion-driven: start with the media budget

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under cost-based pricing?

answers.A: Set price based on the customer’s perceived value (benefits), not the sellers cost
answers.B: Set price based on the costs of producing, distributing, and selling
answers.C: Set price to create a seamless customer experience across touchpoints
answers.D: Set price to introduce new products or build primary demand

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase defines cost-plus pricing?

answers.A: Definition: Price = fixed cost + variable cost only
answers.B: Definition: Price = perceived value + standard markdown
answers.C: Definition: Price = competitor price + ad spend
answers.D: Definition: Price = cost + standard markup

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under cost-plus pricing?

answers.A: Why firms use it: reduces mixed or confusing messages
answers.B: Why firms use it: coordinated and consistent across channels
answers.C: Why firms use it: simple, predictable, feels “fair”
answers.D: Why firms use it: creates one voice across all media

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under new product pricing?

answers.A: Selective distribution
answers.B: Reminder advertising
answers.C: Market-Skimming
answers.D: Trade promotion

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under new product pricing?

answers.A: Interactive Marketing
answers.B: Branded entertainment
answers.C: Straight Product Extension
answers.D: Market Penetration

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under deceptive pricing?

answers.A: good-value pricing: the right mix of quality and service at a fair price
answers.B: fake “regular” prices, drip fees, confusing price representation
answers.C: one clear and consistent brand message
answers.D: same marketing strategy across countries

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines a marketing channel?

answers.A: The promotion mix is the specific blend of promotion tools that the company uses to persuasively communicate customer value and build customer relationships.
answers.B: Marketing channel (distribution channel): A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.
answers.C: Advertising is paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services.
answers.D: Retailing includes all the activities in selling products or services directly to final consumers for their personal, nonbusiness use.

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under why channels add value?

answers.A: Keep the brand in customers’ minds and maintain relationships
answers.B: Match supply with what customers want (right assortment)
answers.C: Introduce new products or build primary demand
answers.D: Build stronger brand awareness

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under why channels add value?

answers.A: Bridge gaps: awareness, interest, and desire
answers.B: Bridge gaps: quality, service, and price
answers.C: Bridge gaps: promotion, segmentation, and positioning
answers.D: Bridge gaps: time, place, and possession

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines channel level?

answers.A: A sales promotion aimed at organizational buyers
answers.B: A group of closely related products sold to similar customers
answers.C: Channel Level: A layer of intermediaries that moves the product and ownership closer to the final buyer.
answers.D: A specific communication task with a defined target audience and time period

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines a direct channel?

answers.A: Retailers are businesses whose sales come primarily from retailing.
answers.B: Direct channel: no intermediaries
answers.C: Indirect channel: one or more intermediaries
answers.D: Inbound logistics (into the company)

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact item is listed as a key channel flow?

answers.A: Brand equity flow
answers.B: Culture flow
answers.C: Experience flow
answers.D: Ownership flow

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under types of VMS?

answers.A: Experiential VMS
answers.B: Reminder VMS
answers.C: Persuasive VMS
answers.D: Corporate VMS

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under types of VMS?

answers.A: Integrated VMS
answers.B: Lifestyle VMS
answers.C: Geographic VMS
answers.D: Contractual VMS

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines marketing logistics?

answers.A: Marketing logistics (physical distribution): planning and managing the flow of goods + information from origin to consumption. Goal: deliver the desired customer service level at the lowest total cost.
answers.B: Marketing logistics means standardizing ads across countries.
answers.C: Marketing logistics means eliminating all intermediaries.
answers.D: Marketing logistics means maximizing delivery speed regardless of cost.

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines retailing?

answers.A: Retailing includes all the activities in selling products or services directly to final consumers for their personal, nonbusiness use.
answers.B: Promotion is directed at final consumers to create demand.
answers.C: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion by an identified sponsor.
answers.D: Wholesaling includes all activities involved in selling goods and services to those buying for resale or business use.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines the promotion mix?

answers.A: Customer insights is a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior.
answers.B: The promotion mix is the specific blend of promotion tools that the company uses to persuasively communicate customer value and build customer relationships.
answers.C: Price is the money charged for a product/service.
answers.D: Marketing channel (distribution channel): A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines advertising?

answers.A: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion of ideas, goods, or services by an identified sponsor.
answers.B: Public relations involves building good relations with the company’s various publics by obtaining favorable publicity, building up a good corporate image, and handling or heading off unfavorable rumors, stories, and events.
answers.C: Sales promotion is a short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service.
answers.D: Personal selling is the personal interaction by the firm’s sales force for the purpose of engaging customers, making sales, and building customer relationships.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines sales promotion?

answers.A: Sales promotion is a short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service.
answers.B: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion of ideas, goods, or services by an identified sponsor.
answers.C: Reminder advertising - keep the brand in customers’ minds and maintain relationships
answers.D: Exporting – selling products produced in one country to buyers in another

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines personal selling?

answers.A: Public relations involves building good relations with the company’s various publics.
answers.B: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion.
answers.C: Sales promotion is a short-term incentive.
answers.D: Personal selling is the personal interaction by the firm’s sales force for the purpose of engaging customers, making sales, and building customer relationships.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines IMC?

answers.A: IMC = choosing the lowest-cost media mix.
answers.B: IMC = selling the same product everywhere.
answers.C: IMC = increasing ad frequency until sales rise.
answers.D: IMC = coordinating communication tools to deliver a clear, consistent, and compelling brand message.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact sentence appears under why it matters?

answers.A: One channel should feel like one retailer, no matter the market.
answers.B: One brand should feel like one voice, no matter where the customer encounters it.
answers.C: One product should feel like one feature set, no matter the segment.
answers.D: One price should feel like one deal, no matter where the customer shops.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact step appears first in developing effective marketing communication?

answers.A: Set communication objectives
answers.B: Design the message
answers.C: Choose communication channels
answers.D: Identify the target audience

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase appears under push and pull?

answers.A: Promotion is directed at regulators to shape policy.
answers.B: Promotion is directed at suppliers to lower input costs.
answers.C: Promotion is directed at final consumers to create demand.
answers.D: Promotion is directed at channel members to move products through the channel.

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines advertising?

answers.A: A short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service
answers.B: Planning and managing the flow of goods + information from origin to consumption
answers.C: Paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services
answers.D: The personal interaction by the firm’s sales force

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears as a purpose of advertising?

answers.A: Inform customers
answers.B: Break bulk into smaller lots
answers.C: Collect → clean → analyze → test → share → act
answers.D: Deliver the desired customer service level at the lowest total cost

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears under key advertising decisions?

answers.A: Set logistics objectives (inbound, outbound, reverse)
answers.B: Set advertising objectives (inform, engage, persuade, remind)
answers.C: Set product objectives (quality, features, design)
answers.D: Set channel objectives (cost, control, adaptability)

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines an advertising objective?

answers.A: A specific logistics task with a defined service level and time period
answers.B: A specific pricing task with a defined target margin and time period
answers.C: A specific communication task with a defined target audience and time period
answers.D: A specific segmentation task with a defined profile and time period

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines informative advertising?

answers.A: Persuasive advertising – build brand preference and encourage switching
answers.B: Reminder advertising - keep the brand in customers’ minds and maintain relationships
answers.C: Informative advertising – introduce new products or build primary demand
answers.D: Press relations – share newsworthy information with media outlets

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears under advertising budget factors?

answers.A: Performance quality
answers.B: Line filling
answers.C: Buyer readiness stage
answers.D: Stage in the product life cycle

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears under digital marketing process?

answers.A: Use digital channels (web, social, mobile)
answers.B: Use only one channel to avoid mixed messages
answers.C: Use only physical retail outlets
answers.D: Use only TV, radio, and print

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines straight product extension?

answers.A: Product Adaptation – modify the product for local preferences
answers.B: Straight Product Extension – sell the same product everywhere
answers.C: Product Invention – create a new product for a specific market
answers.D: Adapted Marketing – adjust marketing for each country
